data.title: Musk's OpenAI Trial Puts Sam Altman And Greg Brockman Under Spotlight
data.description:
Reuters reported the trial record includes internal papers and personal writings used to argue over whether OpenAI drifted from its original nonprofit purpose into a profit-first enterprise. Jury selection is set for Monday, with opening statements expected on Tuesday.

Elon Musks Bold Move Against OpenAI Leadership

Musk's requested remedies go beyond money, targeting OpenAI's chain of command and governance structure. His filing asks a judge to remove Sam Altman from the OpenAI nonprofit board and to push both Altman and Brockman out of officer roles at the for-profit entity.

The damages claim is also enormous, with one figure cited at $150 billion that would flow to OpenAI's charitable arm rather than to Musk personally. Earlier court papers described a separate ceiling of up to $134 billion.

What Will This Trial Mean For AIs Future?

Internal material has entered public view, including a 2017 diary entry from Brockman that reads: "This is the only chance we have to get out from Elon," and, later, "Is he the ‘glorious leader' that I would pick?" Reuters said the disclosures offer a granular look at how AI executives thought about control, incentives, and strategy as OpenAI scaled.

OpenAI's attorneys say Musk is using the lawsuit to gain leverage and to bolster his rival lab, xAI, rather than to protect a mission statement. OpenAI called the case "nothing more than a harassment campaign that's driven by ego, jealousy and a desire to slow down a competitor."

Image: Shutterstock

data.title: Hamilton Lane Shares Slide Following Hunterbrook Short Report
data.description:
• Hamilton Lane stock is testing key support levels. What’s pressuring HLNE?

Allegations Of Inflated Earnings, Day-One Markups

Monday’s report raises significant concerns over the investment firm’s aggressive valuation practices and fee structures within its private equity funds.

Benzinga has reached out to Hamilton Lane for comment regarding the allegations.

According to Hunterbrook, Hamilton Lane is artificially inflating its fee-related earnings through controversial “day-one markups” on secondary assets. The report alleges that approximately one-third of the valuation gains in Hamilton Lane’s Private Assets Fund are driven by these immediate accounting uplifts rather than any actual asset appreciation.

Furthermore, Hunterbrook claims that by collecting regular performance fees on unrealized gains and excluding stock-based compensation from expenses, the firm boosted its reported fee-related earnings by 37%. Without these specific technical adjustments, the firm’s earnings actually would have declined by 16% over the last nine months of 2025.

Redemptions Risk Unraveling Hamilton Lane’s Financial Model

The short seller warns that this financial model relies heavily on a relentless stream of new retail investor capital and could quickly unravel if withdrawal requests increase. Highlighting this specific risk, Hunterbrook noted that one of Hamilton Lane’s evergreen funds already experienced an estimated $172 million net outflow in March alone.

HLNE Shares Slide Monday Afternoon

HLNE Stock Price Activity: Hamilton Lane shares were down 5.67% at $89.46 at market close on Monday, according to Benzinga Pro data.

Image: Shutterstock

data.title: If You Invested $100 In Home Depot Stock 15 Years Ago, You Would Have This Much Today
data.description:

Buying $100 In HD: If an investor had bought $100 of HD stock 15 years ago, it would be worth $889.69 today based on a price of $332.30 for HD at the time of writing.

data.title: Peter Schiff: Trump 'Has More In Common With AOC Than Reagan,' Shouldn't Buy Spirit Airlines
data.description:
Gold enthusiast Peter Schiff said Monday that President Donald Trump has more in common with Rep. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez (D-N.Y.) than Ronald Reagan, after the president floated using taxpayer money to buy Spirit Airlines out of bankruptcy.

If the proposal is sincere, Schiff posted on X, “either Trump doesn’t understand how capitalism works or doesn’t believe in its principles.”

The $500 Million Lifeline

Trump confirmed Thursday from the Oval Office that the government is weighing a taxpayer-funded takeover of the carrier, telling reporters he would “do it to save jobs” at the right price.

A tentative deal would hand Spirit a $500 million loan in exchange for warrants that could give the U.S. government as much as a 90% stake, according to CNBC. Spirit’s lawyer told a New York bankruptcy court Thursday that talks were at an advanced stage.

GOP Joins The Pile-On

Schiff is not the only conservative voice unhappy with the plan. Sen. Ted Cruz (R-Texas) called the bailout “an absolutely TERRIBLE idea” and compared it to the 2008 TARP rescues.

Sen. Tom Cotton (R-Ark.) said that if private investors don’t think Spirit can be run profitably, “I doubt the US Government can either.”

Transportation Secretary Sean Duffy has also reportedly voiced skepticism.

What Prediction Markets Are Saying

JetBlue Airways (NASDAQ:JBLU) was blocked from acquiring Spirit by the Biden administration in 2024, a decision the White House now blames for the airline’s current trouble.

Frontier Group Holdings (NASDAQ:ULCC) has also previously circled the troubled carrier.

Image: Shutterstock
